sections.paragraph:
συναναχέω (Heliod. [III a.d.] ‘to pour on together with’) pass. συναναχυθῆ̣[ν]α̣ι Ac 11:26 v.l. P74 (cp. Tdf. app. for συναχθήναι, which is prob. a lectio facilior, RBorger, GGA 141) fig. be received—DELG s.v. χέω.

Heliod Heliod , III? a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

GGA GGA = Göttingische Gelehrte Anzeigen—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 965
